tasks.title: Add unidentified_issue category + restrict agent_error semantics
tasks.description:
Context: OpenSpec change improve-harness-consistency, tasks.md §10 and Decision 8 in design.md. This is the semantic floor the whole change builds on — later laps depend on CategoryUnidentifiedIssue existing. Today CategoryAgentError does double duty ("agent made a specific mistake" AND "we couldn't classify"); split them.

Outcome: a new CategoryUnidentifiedIssue = "unidentified_issue" exists, wired into all category machinery and mapped to the does-not-freeze FailureAgent class. CategoryAgentError is now reserved for failures where a SPECIFIC agent-level error was extracted. Build + reliability/telemetry tests green.

Files & scope: internal/reliability/category.go (new category constant; categoryDisplayLabels "unidentified issue"; AllCategories; categoryToClass -> FailureAgent; CategoryDisplayLabel; the "nine categories"->"ten" docstring at line 8). internal/reliability/patterns.go ONLY the Priority-5 comment at ~line 296 (// 5. Default agent_error -> unidentified_issue); the Priority-5 return value itself is finalized in the §3 evidence lap. internal/telemetry/newrelic.go: add a defensive "unidentified_issue" case to the newRelicErrorClass switch (~225-245) returning a distinct class ("RallyUnidentifiedIssue"). Audit per 10.7: any existing code using CategoryAgentError as a DEFAULT/FALLBACK switches to CategoryUnidentifiedIssue; keep the text-pattern usages (gemini-cli/antigravity exit-1, opencode bad-request) on CategoryAgentError — those are specific extracted errors. Do NOT touch the runner budget-kill paths here (later lap).

Acceptance: tasks.md §10.9 — AllCategories contains the new category mapping to FailureAgent; categoryDisplayLabels["unidentified_issue"] == "unidentified issue"; Priority 5 comment updated; the telemetry error-class switch has the defensive case. go build ./... && go test ./internal/reliability/... ./internal/telemetry/... green.

tasks.title: ResolvedModel on TryResult + runner telemetry-tag fallback
tasks.description:
Context: tasks.md §2, design Decision 6. The runner telemetry tag collapses to a bare harness (e.g. "codex") whenever a route uses a bare alias, because each executor resolves its default model internally and never reports it back — hiding per-model signal exactly when it matters.

Outcome: agent.TryResult carries a ResolvedModel populated by every executor with the model actually passed to the CLI; the three runner-tag sites use it when non-empty, so bare-alias routes emit runner = "<harness>:<resolved-model>".

Files & scope: add ResolvedModel string to TryResult in internal/agent/executor.go:48 (NOT internal/agent/agent.go, which is an empty package file). Set it in CodexExecutor (codex.go:170-173, the model local after the opts.Model fallback), ClaudeExecutor, OpenCodeExecutor, AntigravityExecutor from each one's resolved-model local. Update the three runner-tag sites internal/relay/runner.go:2107, 2420, 2997 to call telemetry.RunnerLabel(picked.Harness, firstNonEmpty(result.ResolvedModel, picked.Model)) via a small shared helper.

Design: the executor's own model local is authoritative (the session log does NOT carry the resolved model). The route-resolved model stays authoritative when set; ResolvedModel is the fallback for the bare-alias case only.

Acceptance: tasks.md §2.5 — bare-alias route + executor that sets ResolvedModel emits runner = "<harness>:<resolved-model>"; explicit-model route emits the same value as before; empty ResolvedModel falls back to picked.Model. Tests + go build ./... green.

tasks.title: Populate failure_evidence on every classification priority
tasks.description:
Context: tasks.md §3, design Decision 3. Today only Priority-1 (executor evidence) failures carry a failure_evidence block; Priorities 3/4/5 leave it empty and the source tag is hard-coded "executor_evidence". New Relic shows 74/77 captured failures carry a category but no diagnostic context. Depends on the §10 lap (CategoryUnidentifiedIssue for Priority 5).

Outcome: ClassifyError attaches a *FailureEvidence (with a Source) on every priority, plumbed through to BOTH applyEvidenceToFailureState call sites so the emitted failure_evidence.source reflects the path that produced the category.

Files & scope: internal/reliability/patterns.go — add Evidence *FailureEvidence to StrategyDecision; populate Priority 1 (source="executor_evidence" if unset), Priority 3 (CategoryIncompleteFinalization / "agent exited without finalizing" / "dirty_tree", RawSignal from changed paths), Priority 4 (RE-SCAN logLines after a match to extract the matching line; source="text_pattern", Message=pattern.Name), Priority 5 (CategoryUnidentifiedIssue / "unmatched" / "no recognised provider signal", RawSignal from a bounded log tail). internal/reliability/category.go — add Source string to FailureEvidence. ClassifyContext gains ChangedPaths []string. internal/relay/runner.go — populate ChangedPaths from filesChangedList (3239) / preCommitFilesChanged (1935); at line 2258 add `if resetEvidence == nil { resetEvidence = decision.Evidence }`; update applyEvidenceToFailureState (237) to read ev.Source when non-empty (else the caller default); demote applySafeExecErrorEvidence (256) to fire only when BOTH result.Evidence and decision.Evidence are nil.

Design: re-scan for Priority 4 (design Open Q1 resolved: re-scan, do NOT change the Pattern.Match API). All RawSignals bounded to 256 runes via the existing truncateSignal. The single resetEvidence fallback plumbs Priority 3/4/5 evidence into BOTH the try-log span (runner.go:2454) AND the operator-worthy capture path (runner.go:2530) automatically. Do NOT touch runner.go:2479 — that is handoffOnlyAttempted, not an evidence site.

Acceptance: implementation compiles; reliability + relay packages build (go build ./...). Full evidence assertions are the next lap.

tasks.title: Tests: failure_evidence per classification priority
tasks.description:
Context: tasks.md §3.10. This test lap doubles as the review of the high-risk classification plumbing from the previous lap (it touches every ClassifyError priority + both runner evidence sites).

Outcome: each priority emits a RallyFailure whose failure_evidence.source / category / message / raw_signal match the path that produced it.

Files & scope: internal/reliability/*_test.go and the relay runner test files that exercise ClassifyError -> RallyFailure emission. No production changes beyond tiny safe one-liners; anything larger becomes a new focused head lap.

Acceptance: tasks.md §3.10 — Priority 1 -> source "executor_evidence"; Priority 3 -> "dirty_tree" + CategoryIncompleteFinalization; Priority 4 -> "text_pattern" + the pattern's category + the matched line present in raw_signal; Priority 5 -> "unmatched" + CategoryUnidentifiedIssue; raw_signal non-empty for every source; message matches each path's contract; 256-rune bound holds. go test ./internal/reliability/... ./internal/relay/... green.

tasks.title: Verify foundation slice (category floor, ResolvedModel, evidence plumbing)
tasks.description:

Before diffing: identify the intended merge target from the user / PR metadata / repo docs / branch config / recent history — do NOT assume main — then use git diff <target>...HEAD. Identify the first lap/try of THIS batch and treat earlier branch work as pre-existing (flag, don't remove).

Review with depth — "tests green" is not sufficient for classification semantics: trace CategoryUnidentifiedIssue through categoryToClass / AllCategories; trace the StrategyDecision.Evidence population in each ClassifyError priority and confirm the resetEvidence fallback at runner.go:2258 actually reaches BOTH applyEvidenceToFailureState call sites so the emitted failure_evidence.source genuinely differs per priority; trace ResolvedModel set in all four executors and consumed at the three tag sites (bare-alias case produces "<harness>:<model>").

Check off the tasks.md boxes for §2/§3/§10 only for work verified correct AND thorough. For substantive gaps, add a focused head lap (laps add head) rather than fixing large issues inline. Do not rewrite git history; prefer additive/revert commits.

tasks.title: Runner-side try-budget / run-budget exhaustion classification
tasks.description:
Context: tasks.md §5, design Decision 4. Budget-killed tries currently carry an empty failure_category (no telemetry signal). Depends on the §10 lap (category) and the §3 lap (evidence gates).

Outcome: both try-cap-only kills and run-budget kills carry failure_category = unidentified_issue with distinct fail_reasons — UNLESS executor/disk-log evidence already produced a non-empty Category, in which case that is authoritative. FailureClass stays agent-class (does-not-freeze).

Files & scope: internal/relay/runner.go, two sites.
- Try-cap-only kill (timedOut && !runBudgetExhausted): place the override AFTER the runBudgetExhausted reset block (~2311-2323) and OUTSIDE the `if failed && !lapPinMismatch && attemptOutcome.CarriesFailureCategory()` block — that block is SKIPPED here (attemptOutcome = OutcomeRunTimeout, whose CarriesFailureCategory() is false), so ClassifyError never ran and the `decision` local is NOT in scope — do not reference it. Gate: result.Evidence == nil || result.Evidence.Category == "". When it fires: failureCategory = unidentified_issue, failReason = "try budget exhausted; no output", failureClass = FailureAgent.
- Run-budget kill (timedOut && runBudgetExhausted): in the existing block (~2311-2323), change failureCategory = "" to unidentified_issue, gated `if runBudgetExhausted && failureCategory == "" && (result.Evidence == nil || result.Evidence.Category == "")` so a classifier-produced category (e.g. a text-pattern agent_error) stays authoritative. failReason "run timeout" and FailureAgent unchanged.

Design: the result.Evidence == nil || .Category == "" check IS the "real evidence produced" gate. Do NOT duplicate the slow-vs-underqualified diagnostic tags into failure_evidence — they live on the try log already (timeout_kind, runtime_ms, tool_calls, last_output_age_ms).

Acceptance: implementation compiles (go build ./...). Full matrix is the next lap.

tasks.title: Tests: budget-kill classification matrix
tasks.description:
Context: tasks.md §5.5 (six scenarios). Reviews the subtle gate logic from the previous lap.

Files & scope: internal/relay/runner_test.go and siblings. Production fixes limited to tiny safe one-liners; anything larger -> new head lap.

Acceptance: tasks.md §5.5 (a)-(f): (a) try-cap kill, no evidence -> unidentified_issue + "try budget exhausted; no output" + FailureAgent; (b) try-cap kill with executor evidence carrying a non-empty Category (e.g. codex session log -> harness_launch) -> no override, executor authoritative; (c) freeze counter does NOT increment on a try-cap kill; (d) run-budget kill, no evidence/no classifier category -> unidentified_issue + "run timeout" + FailureAgent (changed from the old empty-category behaviour); (e) run-budget kill where ClassifyError produced a category -> classifier authoritative, no override; (f) no regression in operator-worthy capture for any budget-kill path. go test ./internal/relay/... green.

tasks.title: RallyRoute event + NULL-outcome RallyTry cleanup
tasks.description:
Context: tasks.md §7, design Decision 5. 39 routing-fallback events over 30 days are mis-emitted as RallyTry with no outcome, polluting every failure-rate query.

Outcome: a new RallyRoute custom event carries routing decisions; the route-fallback emission moves off RallyTry; every remaining RallyTry emit carries a non-empty outcome; the recovery-cap-hit path keeps its operator-worthy RallyFailure AND additionally emits RallyRoute.

Files & scope:
- internal/telemetry/sink.go (+ NoopSink): add EmitRouteEvent(ctx context.Context, fields map[string]interface{}).
- internal/telemetry/newrelic.go: implement NewRelicSink.EmitRouteEvent via app.RecordCustomEvent("RallyRoute", buildFlatAttributes(fields)).
- internal/telemetry/attributes.go: add RallyRoute to the fixed event-name set.
- internal/telemetry/newrelic_coverage_test.go: add RallyRoute to the allowed event-name map (~146) and bump the count assertion (~154, len != 3 -> != 4).
- internal/relay/runner.go: move the route-fallback emit at runner.go:830 from EmitTryLog to EmitRouteEvent (carry event="route_fallback", from_runner, to_runner, role, lap_id, repo, repo_name, relay_id, run_id, fallback-cause fields); at runner.go:834 KEEP the existing CaptureFailure (operator-worthy needs_user) AND also emit a RallyRoute for audit parity; add a boundary guard in EmitTryLog that logs a warning and fills outcome="unknown" if missing; audit sites 830/2100/2574/3046 (expected: only 830 was missing outcome).

Design: keep RallyTry pure (every event a real try outcome with a non-empty outcome). Do not lose the operator-worthy RallyFailure.

Acceptance: tasks.md §7.8 (a)-(d): route-fallback -> RallyRoute (not RallyTry); recovery-cap-hit -> BOTH RallyRoute and a needs_user RallyFailure; every RallyTry has a non-empty outcome; the boundary guard fires when a caller forgets outcome. go test ./internal/telemetry/... ./internal/relay/... green.

tasks.title: Verify budget classification + RallyRoute telemetry
tasks.description:

Check off §5/§7 tasks.md boxes for verified-correct work; add a focused head lap for any substantive gap. No history rewriting.

tasks.title: Codex session-log fallback (silent exit-1 enrichment)
tasks.description:
Context: tasks.md §4, design Decision 2 + 9. Codex's silent exit-1 burst (0.11.2, 10 failures in one relay) wrote nothing in-band, yet codex keeps rich rollout-*.jsonl session logs we never read. Depends on the §10 lap (category) and the §2 ResolvedModel lap.

Outcome: on a codex error with no parser-matchable in-band signal, enrich from the matching session log (Source="codex_session_log"); when no session log matches, the EXECUTOR returns FailureEvidence{Category: CategoryHarnessLaunch, Source: "codex_no_session_log"} so ClassifyError Priority 1 maps it to FreshRestart/FailureInfra (retry within budget, then freeze pressure after 2+).

Files & scope: internal/agent/codex.go. Locate the latest $CODEX_HOME/sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/rollout-*.jsonl (default ~/.codex/sessions/) whose first-line session_meta.cwd == opts.WorkspaceDir and whose timestamp is within the try window. Read ONLY: first-line scalars (cwd, git.commit_hash, git.branch, model_provider, cli_version) and the last event_msg subtype (task_started / task_complete / turn_aborted) as the diagnostic Message. EXPLICITLY skip base_instructions, token_count, response_item, turn_context. Change the codex.go:195-201 error path: when ParseCodexError is nil and no session log matched, return &TryResult{Evidence: <codex_no_session_log harness_launch evidence>}, execErr instead of return nil, execErr. 256-rune bound via truncateSignal.

Design: do NOT use CategoryAuthOrProxy for the no-session-log case (that maps to Rotate/FailureAgent — wrong; the runner should keep retrying launch failures within budget, hence harness_launch -> FreshRestart). The merged-stderr idiom at codex.go:223 is the standard Go pattern, not a race — no refactor. The resolved model is NOT in session_meta; ResolvedModel comes from the executor's model local (§2), not the log.

Acceptance: tasks.md §4.6 (a)-(d): (a) matching session log -> "codex_session_log" + Message from the last event subtype; (b) no session log -> CategoryHarnessLaunch + "codex_no_session_log" resolving Priority 1 to harness_launch / StrategyFreshRestart / FailureInfra (NOT Rotate/FailureAgent); (c) base_instructions / token_count / turn_context never appear in RawSignal; (d) missing/unreadable session dir is not an error (falls through to the existing safe_exec_error path). Tests green.

tasks.title: OpenCode budget-kill disk-log fallback (extend existing machinery)
tasks.description:
Context: tasks.md §6, design Decision 9. OpenCode ALREADY has a disk-log fallback (attachOpenCodeFailureEvidence and friends, opencode.go:120-263) that today only extracts usage-limit / stream-error. EXTEND it — do not stand up a parallel mechanism. Depends on the §5 budget-kill lap (the override respects a non-empty Category).

Outcome: budget-killed opencode tries with no parseable output get Source="opencode_disk_log" evidence with a content-appropriate Category and a bounded structural tail.

Files & scope: internal/agent/opencode.go. The existing runErr != nil path already triggers attachOpenCodeFailureEvidence for budget-killed tries (exec.CommandContext kill -> runErr; openCodeNeedsFailureEvidence true; parseOpenCodeOutput always returns non-nil TryResult) — NO new RunOptions flag needed; the gap is the filtering. Extend the filtering for the budget-killed case to ALSO keep level=WARN and level=ERROR lines plus structural message=created / message="loop session.id=" / message=stream markers, bounded to 16 lines; explicitly skip per-token / per-tool-call / per-permission lines. Category by content: recognisable error shape -> recognised category (usage_limit, auth_or_proxy, ...); parseable WARN/ERROR but no recognisable shape -> agent_error; only structural markers -> unidentified_issue (Message "try budget exhausted; no parseable output"). UPDATE the precedence at opencode.go:128-133: disk-log evidence NEVER replaces in-band evidence that already has a non-empty Category (the current `== CategoryAgentError` check is too permissive after the stricter agent_error semantics). 256-rune bound.

Acceptance: tasks.md §6.5 (a)-(f): recognisable error -> correct recognised category; WARN/ERROR no shape -> agent_error with error lines in RawSignal; only structural -> unidentified_issue + "try budget exhausted; no parseable output"; per-token lines never in RawSignal; 256-rune bound holds; existing usage-limit extraction still works (regression guard). Tests green.

tasks.title: Claude session-JSONL disk-log fallback
tasks.description:
Context: tasks.md §11, design Decision 9. Claude keeps per-session JSONL at ~/.claude/projects/<project>/<sessionId>.jsonl; Rally already passes --resume <sessionId> so the UUID is known at the executor. The user event's display field holds the FULL prompt — the single largest telemetry-leak risk. Depends on the §10 lap.

Outcome: on a claude failure with no in-band evidence, enrich from the session JSONL (Source="claude_session_log") with a recognised Category or unidentified_issue.

Files & scope: internal/agent/claude.go. Build the path from the "-"-normalised opts.WorkspaceDir + sessionId (Claude's project-path convention). Read the last ~50 lines; keep error-shaped events: assistant events with message.stop_reason == "error" (or "end_turn"), system events with subtype "init" (startup errors). EXPLICITLY skip user events (full prompt in display), thinking events, and tool_result bodies beyond their first line. Map known stop reasons/messages (overloaded_error -> provider_overloaded, authentication_error -> auth_or_proxy); otherwise unidentified_issue. Wire after the existing ParseClaudeError attempt, following the opencode precedence pattern (opencode.go:131). 256-rune bound.

Acceptance: tasks.md §11.6 (a)-(e): (a) stop_reason "end_turn", no error -> no fallback evidence; (b) stop_reason "error" + overloaded -> "claude_session_log" + provider_overloaded; (c) unrecognised error text -> "claude_session_log" + unidentified_issue; (d) user event display fields NEVER appear in RawSignal; (e) missing/unreadable session file is not an error (falls through to ParseClaudeError on in-band output). Tests green.

tasks.title: Antigravity glog disk-log fallback
tasks.description:
Context: tasks.md §12, design Decision 9. Antigravity keeps glog at ~/.gemini/antigravity-cli/log/cli-*.log; Rally already reads its --log-file temp output (antigravity.go:99-101) but not the persistent glog. Credential files live in the same tree and must never be read. Depends on the §10 lap.

Outcome: on an antigravity failure with no in-band evidence, enrich from the latest cli-*.log (Source="antigravity_glog") with a recognised Category or unidentified_issue.

Files & scope: internal/agent/antigravity.go. Find the latest cli-*.log by mtime. Read the last ~1MB; glog lines are prefixed I/W/E/F + MMDD HH:MM:SS.uuuuuu. Keep ONLY E-prefixed lines + up to 3 context lines each (one before, two after). EXPLICITLY skip I- and W-prefixed lines and ANY line containing oauth_creds.json / antigravity-oauth-token / settings.json. NEVER read oauth_creds.json or settings.json directly. Map known errors ("not logged into Antigravity" / "error getting token source" -> auth_or_proxy; "RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED" / "429" -> usage_limit or short_rate_limit); otherwise unidentified_issue. Wire after the existing temp --log-file read + parse call. 256-rune bound.

Design note: the reliability parser rename ParseGeminiError -> ParseAntigravityError happens in the LATER gemini-cut lap (task 1.3). At THIS lap the function is still named ParseGeminiError — call whatever name currently exists in the tree; the gemini-cut lap updates the caller.

Acceptance: tasks.md §12.6 (a)-(f): (a) E-prefixed auth error -> "antigravity_glog" + auth_or_proxy; (b) unrecognised E error -> "antigravity_glog" + unidentified_issue; (c) I-prefixed lines never in RawSignal; (d) credential file paths never in RawSignal; (e) missing/unreadable glog dir is not an error; (f) the existing temp --log-file reading at antigravity.go:99-101 still works (regression guard). Tests green.

tasks.title: Verify unified disk-log fallback contract (all four harnesses)
tasks.description:
OpenSpec verification scoped to tasks.md §4, §6, §11, §12 (codex / opencode / claude / antigravity disk-log fallbacks). Use openspec-verify-change against improve-harness-consistency.

Identify the merge target (not assumed main) and the first lap of this batch before diffing; treat earlier branch work as pre-existing.

The contract to confirm across all four executors: every disk-log fallback yields a non-nil FailureEvidence with a non-empty Category (recognised or unidentified_issue), the correct Source marker (codex_session_log / codex_no_session_log / opencode_disk_log / claude_session_log / antigravity_glog), a <=256-rune RawSignal, and — the headline risk — NO prompt / credential / token-payload content. Read the actual skip-lists in each parser (codex base_instructions+token_count+turn_context; claude user+thinking+tool_result bodies; antigravity I/W lines + credential paths; opencode per-token/per-permission), not just that tests pass. Confirm the codex no-session-log path resolves to harness_launch/FreshRestart/FailureInfra (not Rotate).

Check off §4/§6/§11/§12 tasks.md boxes for verified-correct, PII-safe work; add a focused head lap for any gap. No history rewriting.

tasks.title: Fix OpenCode in-band precedence for server-log evidence
tasks.description:
Verification found a contract gap in improve-harness-consistency task 6.4.

Context: openspec/changes/improve-harness-consistency/tasks.md 6.4 and specs/executor/spec.md "Disk log fallback does not override in-band evidence" require server-log-derived evidence to NEVER replace in-band Evidence when the in-band path already produced a non-empty Category. Current code in internal/agent/opencode.go:136-146 still does exactly that for the legacy stream-error path: if ParseOpencodeError returns CategoryAgentError from stdout/stderr and openCodeServerLogFailureEvidence finds usage_limit in the server log, attachOpenCodeFailureEvidence overwrites the in-band evidence. The regression guard at internal/agent/agent_test.go:1541-1578 codifies that override.

Outcome: align OpenCode with the unified disk-log contract. Server-log evidence must not replace non-empty in-band Evidence. If preserving the usage-limit regression surface still matters, narrow the generic in-band ParseOpencodeError path so wrapper-only UnknownError output does not claim a specific Category before the server-log path runs. Update tests so they assert both halves of the contract: (a) no override once in-band Evidence has a real Category, and (b) usage-limit extraction still works when the in-band path has no usable category.

tasks.title: Re-verify OpenCode disk-log contract after precedence fix
tasks.description: OpenSpec re-verification for improve-harness-consistency task 6.4 after work-4106. Confirm the real executor path in internal/agent/opencode.go now satisfies the full disk-log contract, not just the helper-level tests: (1) server-log-derived evidence NEVER replaces non-empty in-band Evidence; (2) the recognized stream-error/server-log path also emits Source = "opencode_disk_log" and a bounded filtered-tail RawSignal, instead of the legacy empty-Source ParseOpencodeError result from openCodeEvidenceFromServerLog; (3) budget-killed recognized usage-limit/auth shapes still classify correctly; (4) per-token/per-permission noise still stays out of RawSignal. Re-check openspec/changes/improve-harness-consistency/tasks.md section 6 and only mark 6.4 done once the executor-level path matches specs/executor/spec.md "Disk log fallback does not override in-band evidence" end to end.

tasks.title: Cut the gemini CLI harness (BREAKING) — removal + compile-fixing tests + resolution warning
tasks.description:
Context: tasks.md §1, design Decision 1 + 7. Ships LAST per the migration plan. gemini is upstream-deprecated; antigravity becomes the only Google-owned harness. THE TRAP: cfg.ResolveAgent consults the production builtInAliases map at config_v2.go:47-48, NOT the route_runtime/mix tables — miss it and `ge:pro` still resolves to a deleted executor and only fails late at runner.go:3159 with "no executor for agent gemini".

Outcome: all gemini production surface removed; ge/gemini aliases fail to resolve with a one-time warning naming role/route/alias and recommending antigravity; ResolveAgent is the warning hook; the ui role default route becomes ag; go build ./... and go vet ./... pass.

Files & scope (production + compile-fixing tests):
- Delete internal/agent/gemini.go + GeminiExecutor refs in cmd/rally/main.go (1.1); remove gemini executor tests AND every GeminiExecutor literal/map entry in internal/agent/agent_test.go so it compiles (1.2, 1.12).
- Rename ParseGeminiError -> ParseAntigravityError in reliability/antigravity.go:23 + the surviving caller agent/antigravity.go:106 + antigravity_test.go + regression_test.go (1.3).
- Pattern surgery in reliability/patterns.go: REMOVE ONLY the Harness:"gemini" "gemini auth or unsupported client" pattern (237-248); KEEP and RENAME the antigravity-scoped "gemini-cli exit 1" pattern (223-236, Harness:"antigravity") to "antigravity gemini-cli exit 1" — do NOT delete it; leave the antigravity eligibility pattern (249-260) unchanged (1.4).
- Drop ge/gemini aliases from route_runtime.go:715, mix.go:97 + their tests (1.5).
- config/config_v2.go: remove GeminiModel everywhere (defaults 80, structs 194 & 223, merge table 455, accessor 636-637, projection 923) AND the builtInAliases ge/gemini entries (47-48) AND the harness-canonical entry (57) AND lines 67 & 71; remove GeminiModel reads in cmd/rally/init_roles.go (~140-141); update config_v2_test.go (1.6).
- Remove gemini from providers.go:511 and the routes_check.go:441 allowlist (1.7); remove the gemini_model huh.NewInput prompt in cli/config.go:111 + fixtures (1.8).
- Replace the ui role Route []string{"gemini"} at init_roles.go:65 with []string{"ag"} (1.9) — do NOT leave it unrouted.
- Add the one-time resolution-failure warning at cfg.ResolveAgent (config_v2.go:647): after the builtInAliases removal, ge/gemini falls through to the unknown-alias path; recognise those historical inputs explicitly and emit the upgrade guidance (role + route entry + alias -> antigravity) before returning the unresolvable-route error (1.14). See spec cli-config "Removed gemini harness alias warning".

Acceptance: go build ./... && go vet ./... green. go test ./... may still show fixture failures from the broader scrub (next lap) but MUST compile.

tasks.title: Gemini cut cleanup — fixture scrub + README + full green
tasks.description:

- 9.4 bare-alias codex route -> runner = "codex:<model>".

tasks.description:
Context: Phase 2, task 3.1 of `slim-cli-composition-root`. Outcome: Release/update metadata no longer depends on the app layer; `internal/release` does not import `internal/app`.

Files & scope: Move `BinaryName`, `ReleaseOwner`, `ReleaseRepo`, and `EnvNoUpdateCheck` from `internal/app` to `internal/release` (release.go). Re-point all callers (process entry / CLI / update-check) to use `release.*`. Do not move anything else out of `internal/app` in this lap.

Design: The constants are product/release metadata, so `internal/release` is their correct home; this also keeps the future `app.StartRelay` seam reusable without dodging the cycle via a different package. Verify the direction with `go list -f '{{.Imports}}' ./internal/release` after the move — it must not list `internal/app`.

Acceptance: `internal/release` no longer imports `internal/app`; all former `app.BinaryName`/`app.ReleaseOwner`/`app.ReleaseRepo`/`app.EnvNoUpdateCheck` references now resolve to `release.*`; `go build ./...` compiles; `go test -count=1 ./internal/release ./cmd/rally` green.

tasks.title: Extract BuildExecutors and relocate commitSetupFiles to gitx
tasks.description:
Context: Phase 2, tasks 3.2 and 3.3 of `slim-cli-composition-root`. Two self-contained verbatim lifts out of the relay command / main package, done together because both are mechanical extractions of composition helpers feeding the seam.

Outcome: The executor registry and the setup-file commit helper live in their target packages with call sites re-pointed; behaviour unchanged.

Files & scope:
- 3.2 Add `app.BuildExecutors(cfg config.V2Config) map[string]agent.Executor` in `internal/app/executors.go`: lift the built-in (`antigravity`/`claude`/`codex`/`opencode`) + generic-harness registry construction verbatim out of `runRelay`, and point the (still `package main`) relay command at it. Same harness set and configuration as the inline map.
- 3.3 Relocate `commitSetupFiles` → `internal/gitx` as `gitx.CommitSetupFiles(workspaceDir string, paths []string, message string) (bool, error)` (the path-scoped `--no-verify` setup commit). Move its tests from `cmd/rally/main_test.go` into `internal/gitx`. Update BOTH CLI-side call sites: init workspace-setup files, and laps-hook install.

Design: Verbatim lifts; no logic change. `BuildExecutors` is deliberately a thin helper here (change #4 re-homes it into a real harness registry). Mark off this helper in the 1.4 migration checklist so the `commitSetupFiles` test is accounted for exactly once.

Acceptance: `go test -count=1 ./internal/app ./internal/gitx ./cmd/rally` green; `go build ./...` compiles; the relay command builds executors via `app.BuildExecutors` and both setup-commit call sites use `gitx.CommitSetupFiles`.

tasks.title: Verify Phase 1 splits + edge break + helper extractions
tasks.description:

tasks.title: Add non-mutating app.InspectResume with layout-migration coverage
tasks.description:
Context: Phase 2, task 3.4 of `slim-cli-composition-root`. The CLI must prompt resume-vs-new BEFORE `app.StartRelay` opens the store for the run, but `internal/app` must not import `internal/user_prompt`. `InspectResume` is the non-mutating peek that gives the CLI the unfinished-relay summary to prompt with.

Outcome: `app.InspectResume(workspaceDir) (app.ResumeInfo, error)` returns the most-recent-unfinished-relay summary using the same store init/migration path as startup, without mutating relay or agent status.

Files & scope: Add `internal/app/relay_start.go` (or start it here) with `ResumeInfo{HasUnfinished, RelayID, CompletedIterations, TargetIterations, AgentMix}` and `InspectResume`. Open the store via the same `store.NewStore(store.RallyDir(workspaceDir))` initialization/migration path current startup uses.

Design: “Non-mutating” means no relay completion and no agent-status reset — NOT bypassing the existing on-open layout migration. Legacy top-level `.rally/relays.jsonl` state must be visible through this peek (the migration runs as it would at startup). Keep `internal/app` free of `internal/user_prompt` and `internal/laps`.

Acceptance: Unit-test `InspectResume` two ways: (a) a temp workspace seeded with a current-layout store holding an unfinished relay, and (b) a legacy top-level `.rally/relays.jsonl` layout — both must surface the unfinished relay and its summary, and neither may complete the relay or reset status. `go test -count=1 ./internal/app ./cmd/rally` green; `go build ./...` compiles.

tasks.title: Extract the runtime relay-start mechanics into app.StartRelay
tasks.description:
Context: Phase 2, task 3.5 of `slim-cli-composition-root` — the highest-risk lap. Lift the lower-level runtime mechanics out of the ~295-line `runRelay` into a presentation-neutral `app.StartRelay`, leaving interactive prompting in the (still `package main`) command for now (the next lap rewires the command top-half).

Outcome: `app.StartRelay(ctx, app.RelayStartOptions) error` turns already-resolved inputs into a configured `runner.Runner` and runs it; the relay command delegates the runtime portion to it.

Files & scope: In `internal/app/relay_start.go` add `RelayStartOptions` (per design.md: WorkspaceDir, Config, TaskPrompt, AgentMixSpecs, UsedOverride, TargetIters, LapsEnabled, DataDir, Telemetry, and the resolved decision fields DiscardUnfinishedRelay/ResetAgentStatus/OverwriteMixOnResume, plus Out/Err writers), `TelemetryBuild`, `telemetryConfigForRelay` (moved alongside, fed by `opts.Telemetry`), and `StartRelay`. Move verbatim out of `runRelay`: rally-initialized check / store open, new-batch/discard handling from the resolved booleans, mix-overwrite application, `app.BuildExecutors`, `telemetry.InitWithIdentity`, `BuildProviderIndex`, `runner.Config` assembly + resolver closure + override-route validation + instructions read, `runner.NewRunner` + `SetTelemetry`, the double-Ctrl+C signal handling window, and `Runner.Run`.

Design: HARD constraints — keep the telemetry result LOCAL to `StartRelay`: pass `telemetryResult.MachineID` into `runner.Config` and call `r.SetTelemetry(telemetryResult.Sink)`; do NOT rely on any main-package `activeTelemetry`/`activeMachineID` globals after extraction. `internal/app` MUST NOT directly import `internal/user_prompt` or `internal/laps`. Build vars arrive only via `opts.Telemetry` (threaded from `package main`); do not redeclare them in `internal/app`. `app` may write to `Out`/`Err` but must never read input to branch.

Acceptance: `go build ./...` compiles; `go test -count=1 ./internal/app ./cmd/rally` green; `go list -f '{{.Imports}}' ./internal/app` lists neither `internal/user_prompt` nor `internal/laps`. (Full unit tests for StartRelay come in the dedicated test lap.)

tasks.title: Rewire the relay command to the seam, preserving resume semantics
tasks.description:
Context: Phase 2, task 3.6 of `slim-cli-composition-root`. With `StartRelay` extracted, reorganize the (still `package main`) relay command into a top-to-bottom script that resolves every interactive decision CLI-side and hands `app.StartRelay` fully-resolved options. Resume/mix semantics are subtle and must be preserved exactly.

Outcome: The relay command reads as a script: parse flags → resolve workspace → `config.LoadV2` + print deprecation notes → laps warning/detect → default iterations (laps-aware) → `chooseRelayAgentSpecs` → `cli.ValidateRelayStartupRoutes` → initialized-workspace check → `syncRoleFolders` → when laps enabled: `laps.InstallHooks` + `gitx.CommitSetupFiles` → `app.InspectResume` + resume/mix `user_prompt` decisions → build `RelayStartOptions` → `app.StartRelay`.

Files & scope: The relay command file under `cmd/rally/` (still `package main` this phase). Laps hook install stays CLI-side; `app` only receives the resolved `LapsEnabled`.

Design: PRESERVE these exact semantics — `--new` completes the unfinished relay AND resets agent status (DiscardUnfinishedRelay=true, ResetAgentStatus=true); interactive “Start new” completes the unfinished relay but does NOT reset agent status (DiscardUnfinishedRelay=true, ResetAgentStatus=false); non-interactive `--resume --agent ...` does NOT set OverwriteMixOnResume unless a separate change intentionally alters it. Map each prompt outcome to the resolved boolean fields on `RelayStartOptions`. Do not change any command name, flag, help text, or printed output.

Acceptance: `go build ./...` compiles; `go test -count=1 ./cmd/rally ./internal/app` green; observable relay-start behaviour (resume/new/mix prompts and their effects) is identical to pre-change `runRelay`.

tasks.title: Unit-test app.StartRelay and assert the seam invariants
tasks.description:
Context: Phase 2, tasks 3.7 and 3.8 of `slim-cli-composition-root`. Dedicated test lap that doubles as a thorough review of the high-risk `StartRelay` extraction and the import-boundary invariant.

Outcome: `app.StartRelay` has focused unit tests driven entirely by resolved options (no stdin reaches `app`), and the app-layer import invariant is asserted.

Files & scope: Tests under `internal/app`. Use a temp workspace and captured `Out`/`Err` writers; assert no prompting path exists in `app`.

Design: Cover the resolved-decision matrix that `StartRelay` consumes (discard/reset/overwrite-mix combinations as far as is testable without a live runner), telemetry result staying local, and that the signal-handling goroutine is race-clean. 3.8 invariant: `go list -f '{{.Imports}}' ./internal/app` contains neither `internal/user_prompt` nor `internal/laps`; `go list -deps ./internal/app` may contain `internal/laps` transitively via `runner` but MUST complete without an import cycle.

Acceptance: `go test -count=1 ./internal/app ./cmd/rally` green; `go test -race -count=1 ./internal/app` green (signal-handling goroutine); the 3.8 import assertions hold.

tasks.title: Verify the presentation-neutral app seam (Phase 2)

tasks.title: Introduce cli.NewRootCommand and RootOptions
tasks.description:
Context: Phase 3, task 4.1 of `slim-cli-composition-root` — the keystone of the CLI layer. Establish the root-command builder that `cmd/rally/main.go` will collapse onto; subsequent laps move the individual handlers into `internal/cli` to register here.

Outcome: `cli.NewRootCommand(opts RootOptions) *cobra.Command` constructs the full command tree, beside the existing `NewConfigCmd`/`NewRoutesCmd`/`NewHooksCmd`.

Files & scope: `internal/cli/root.go`. Add `RootOptions` carrying the build vars (`Version` + `NewRelic{LicenseKey,AppName,HostDisplayName}`) threaded from `package main`. `NewRootCommand` registers `start`/`relay`, `init`, hidden `init-roles`, `instructions`, `routes`, `hooks`, `config`, `version`, `update`, `progress`, and `tail`; wires the `--version`/`-v` flag; and installs the laps-aware dynamic help func (hides `progress` when laps is detected). Thread the `opts` build vars into the telemetry config eventually passed to `app.StartRelay`.

Design: This lap may temporarily delegate to handlers still defined in `package main` (call across the import edge as needed) so it compiles before the next laps physically move them — OR move them together if cleaner; either way preserve exact command names/flags/help text and the dynamic-help behaviour. Do not redeclare build vars in `internal/cli`; they live in `package main` and arrive via `RootOptions`.

Acceptance: `go build ./...` compiles; `go test -count=1 ./internal/cli ./cmd/rally` green; the constructed root registers exactly the same command set, flags, and help behaviour as before (no command/flag/help diff).

tasks.title: Slim cmd/rally/main.go to a process entry point

tasks.title: Resolve the pre-existing gofmt...
tasks.description: Resolve the pre-existing gofmt issue in internal/relay/relay_test.go so gofmt -l . returns empty output, then rerun lap rall-e7b0 to scaffold tools/archguard.

tasks.title: Scaffold tools/archguard: walk, parse, line count, run modes, policy seam
tasks.description:
Context: First lap of OpenSpec change `add-architecture-guardrails` (#3 in the architecture sequence). Tooling-and-CI only — no Rally runtime change, no version bump, no release. Read `openspec/changes/add-architecture-guardrails/{proposal,design,tasks}.md` and `specs/architecture-guardrails/spec.md`. Dependencies #1 (decompose-relay-runner) and #2 (slim-cli-composition-root) have landed and the tree matches design.md's baseline (`internal/relay/runner/run_one.go` 1510, `internal/agent/opencode.go` 801). This lap builds the checker's foundation; the concrete rule classes land in later laps.

Outcome: A new `tools/archguard` command (`package main`, existing module, stdlib-only) that walks the repo, parses each `.go` file with `parser.ImportsOnly`, counts physical lines, and exposes a unit-testable policy-engine seam plus the three run modes — even before any rule is populated.

Files & scope: Create `tools/archguard/**` only (main + policy seam + tests + testdata). Do not touch any `cmd/rally` or `internal/*` runtime file, `go.mod`, or `go.sum`.

Design: Follow design.md Decisions 1 & 2. Stdlib only (`go/parser`, `go/token`, `go/ast`, `os`, `path/filepath`, `bufio`, `strings`, `flag`, `fmt`, `sort`) so `go mod tidy` stays a no-op. The walk skips `// Code generated` files, `testdata`, `vendor`, build output, and hidden dirs (`.git`, `.rally`, `.laps`). Physical lines = newline count (matches `wc -l`). Make the policy engine a testable unit (exported funcs or a `policy` sub-package) independent of the walk/print. Run modes: default (warnings + hard, non-zero exit on hard), `--report` (paste-ready over-budget list plus import/dep violations), `--ci` (hard-only exit; warnings print but never fail). Every violation carries a human-readable architectural reason (design.md Diagnostics format). Keep archguard's own files under budget.

Acceptance: First confirm the tree is green (task 1.1): `go build ./...`, `go vet ./...`, `gofmt -l .` empty, `go test -count=1 ./...`. If red, STOP and surface it — do not fold unrelated fixes into this tooling change. Then: `go mod tidy` produces no diff; unit tests cover line counting, generated-file exemption (`// Code generated`), hidden-dir/`testdata`/`vendor` skipping, and import parsing (aliases, dot-imports, grouped blocks) against `tools/archguard/testdata` fixtures; all three modes run without panicking; `go vet ./...` and `gofmt -l .` clean. Reports tasks 1.1, 2.1, 2.2, 2.4 and the 2.3 engine seam — do not tick tasks.md.

tasks.title: File-size budgets with grandfathered baseline
tasks.description:
Context: Phase 2 of `add-architecture-guardrails`, building on lap 1's policy-engine seam. Read design.md Decision 3 and the file-size scenarios in `specs/architecture-guardrails/spec.md`.

Outcome: The checker enforces file-size budgets with a grandfather map regenerated from HEAD, and `archguard --report` prints the current over-budget files as a paste-ready map. The baseline is green by construction.

Files & scope: `tools/archguard/**` only.

Design: production `.go` warn 500 / hard 800; `_test.go` warn 700 / hard 1000; generated exempt (must carry `// Code generated`). Grandfather semantics: a file in the map is exempt from the standard hard budget but FAILS if it exceeds its OWN recorded cap; a file NOT in the map fails if it exceeds the standard hard budget (that is how a new oversize file is caught). Warnings (500/700) print but NEVER set the exit code. Regenerate the size baseline against HEAD (task 1.2) via `archguard --report` — do NOT trust the 2026-07-01 figures in design.md; caps = current actual line counts. Diagnostics per design.md format (report size vs cap; “ratchet the cap down, never up”).

Acceptance: Paste the regenerated grandfather map; `archguard --ci` exits 0 on the clean tree; a scratch one-line bump to a grandfathered file makes `--ci` fail (then revert). Bundle unit tests + size testdata fixtures: a new oversize non-grandfathered file fails with file+count+budget; a grandfathered file over its cap fails with size-vs-cap; a 500–800 non-grandfathered file warns but `--ci` exits 0; the clean baseline passes. Reports tasks 1.2, 3.1, 3.2 — do not tick tasks.md.

tasks.title: Phase 4: harness registry, app mapper, and removal of internal/agent
tasks.description:
Context: modularize-harness-adapters tasks.md section 5 (5.1-5.3); design.md Decision 5 and the harness-module-structure + composition-root-structure specs. This introduces the only genuinely new logic in the change (the construction registry and the config translation) and removes the emptied internal/agent. Depends on Phases 1-3.

Outcome: internal/harness.BuildExecutors(Config) map[string]harnessapi.Executor builds the four built-ins by canonical name plus one generic adapter per custom entry; harness.Config and GenericConfig are defined; internal/app.BuildExecutors becomes a thin config.V2Config to harness.Config mapper preserving the exact prior executor set and keys; internal/agent is removed with no shim; tree green and no internal/agent import remains.

Files & scope:
- internal/harness/registry.go (package harness): BuildExecutors(cfg Config); Config{ClaudeModel, CodexModel, OpenCodeModel, AntigravityModel string; Custom map[string]GenericConfig}; GenericConfig{Command []string; ModelFlag *string; OutputStrategy string; OutputLines int; TailStream string; Model string}. Construct claude.New/codex.New/opencode.New/antigravity.New keyed by canonical name plus generic.New per Custom entry. This package MUST NOT import internal/config.
- Rewrite internal/app/executors.go: translate config.V2Config (built-in model fields plus cfg.Harnesses command specs) into harness.Config and delegate to harness.BuildExecutors. Preserve ModelFlag *string absent-vs-empty semantics (nil distinct from empty string); leave GenericConfig.Model empty because the config shape has no generic-harness default-model field today. The returned map still feeds runner.NewRunner at the composition root.
- Remove internal/agent entirely. Confirm git grep finds no production import of internal/agent.

Design: This is where subtle parity lives. The pre-change and post-change executor maps must have identical keys and identical per-adapter model wiring; ModelFlag nil vs empty-string must survive the translation; the canonical names claude/codex/opencode/antigravity are unchanged. The registry is the only harness-layer package that imports the adapter subpackages.

Acceptance: go build ./...; go test -count=1 ./internal/app ./internal/relay/... ./cmd/rally; go vet; gofmt clean; git grep -n 'internal/agent' shows no import (docs/spec references excepted). archguard --ci still RED until Phase 5. Do not tick tasks.md.
